experiment: Open-source BF16 GEMM scaling (IRON+Peano, no Chess)
kernel: aie::mmul<8,8,8,bf16,bf16,accfloat> with mac() accumulate, K-dim tiling (KI=2)
configuration: M=8, K=16 (2 K-tiles of 8), N=sweep, tiles=8 (1x8 grid), stack_size=0x600
conclusion: Open-source BF16 GEMM achieves 11.4 GFLOPS on 8 AIE2P tiles. Performance scales linearly with N (compute-bound regime). The Chess reference (30.1 TFLOPS, 32 tiles) uses the same aie::mmul intrinsics compiled through Chess instead of Peano.
